steps.title: Recognize the economic development factor
steps.content: The passage explicitly identifies Serbia as having a “developing economy” right before stating the researchers' caution against generalizing. This placement is the key signal: economic development level likely explains why the findings might not apply universally.

steps.title: Determine what the caution suggests
steps.content:
A caution against generalizing from one economic context logically suggests the need for research across different economic levels to determine whether the finding holds broadly.

Choice A directly proposes this: additional research with countries at varying economic development.

  • B speculates about richer countries' preferences rather than addressing the methodological gap
  • C misidentifies the issue as sample size (unsupported)
  • D shifts to business strategy instead of addressing the researchers' concern
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
